_comment: Template documenting the JSON Claude returns when reviewing a comms/requests/REQ-*.json health-review request. The reviewer pastes a populated copy of this object into the request's free-text answer; the Telegram bot then files it under .response.answers[0].free_text in the standard way (see comms/schema/response.schema.json). This file is documentation only — it is not validated as a JSON Schema.

data_validity.note: DB values sane: no negative sizes, no null required fields, monotonic timestamps, pnl reconciles

trainer_models.note: per-model roll-up; see model_status[] for the per-model detail. concern only when an advisory+/live model degrades on live/shadow data

anomalies: free-form list of notable items the reviewer flagged

backlog_drain._comment: Result of draining docs/claude/health-review-backlog.json this run. One entry per backlog item triaged. action: fixed (resolved in this review's PR-less repo edits) | deferred (still open, restated) | invalid (no longer applies, closed). The skill edits the backlog file itself; this array records what it did.

sprint_doc_review._comment: Recent sprint logs reviewed for doc correctness/clarity since the last review. One entry per issue found (empty array = sprints read, nothing wrong). severity: nit | drift | contradiction. Contradictions against canonical docs are fixed in-place or logged to the backlog.

trade_decision_grades._comment: One entry per closed or rejected trade in the SINCE-LAST-REVIEW window (not a fixed 6h window). Required field; pass [] only when the window genuinely contained zero trades. Each entry is ALSO appended (keyed by trade_id) to comms/claude_trade_scores.jsonl — the durable, trainer-ingestible score log (operator decision 2026-05-24). Letter grade is independent of dollar P&L — small wins on bad setups still grade poorly, stop-outs on textbook setups still grade fairly. The three categorical labels (entry_quality, exit_quality, risk_management) are the training-friendly fields; the letter grade is a rolled-up summary. See .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md § 'Per-trade decision grading' for the full rubric.
trade_decision_grades.entry_quality: optimal | acceptable | late | early | should_skip | unknown
trade_decision_grades.exit_quality: optimal | tp_appropriate | sl_appropriate | premature_exit | held_too_long | unknown
trade_decision_grades.risk_management: correct | oversize | undersize | sl_too_tight | sl_too_wide | unknown
trade_decision_grades.rationale: <= 240 chars — why this grade given signal_logic + outcome
trade_decision_grades.alternative_action: <= 160 chars — what we'd do differently next time, or 'none'

model_status._comment: REQUIRED — one entry per model in `python -m ml list-models` (the registry). A standing status update: each model's latest training-session result, and its shadow/live track record from trade data WHEN AVAILABLE. [] only when the trainer relay errored (then findings.trainer_models = skip). See .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md § 'Model status report'.
model_status.stage: research_only | candidate | backtest_approved | shadow | advisory | limited_live | live_approved

live_shadow.score_summary: score distribution / mean when predictions>0, else 'no predictions yet'
live_shadow.realized: win-rate/PnL of the closed trades this model scored, when joinable; else 'n/a'

model_status.note: <= 160 chars — one-line health verdict for this model
